project: badenleg municipality demand capture and outreach pack
overview: Turn resident demand capture into a municipality-ready outreach and operator workflow. The goal is to connect public data and SEO traffic to verified resident demand, municipality prioritization, and concrete next actions that support first active LEGs.
goals: Measure verified resident demand in a way the operator can act on.
Prioritize municipalities using demand plus current public-data heuristics.
Generate a municipality outreach pack from real demand context.
Keep the work parallelizable across data, funnel, admin, and outreach lanes.
nonGoals: Treating GitHub Issues as the internal kanban.
successMetrics: Operator can identify municipalities with verified resident demand from product data.
Outreach generation has access to a municipality demand snapshot or summary.
Admin/operator surface shows demand, formation pipeline, and next-action context.
Stable Ralph stories can be mirrored cleanly to GitHub Issues for visibility.
openQuestions: What exact threshold should count as verified resident demand for a municipality?
Which existing resident events are reliable enough to seed the first version of the signal?
uiNotes: Operator/admin surfaces should prefer ranked lists, status summaries, and next actions over generic analytics dumps.
Public/municipality messaging should reinforce municipality enablement and resident demand, not speculative roadmap themes.

dataModel.name: municipality demand snapshot
dataModel.description: Per-municipality rollup of resident demand and formation signal used in admin and outreach workflows.

rules: Respect `prd/feature-discipline.md` before creating or running stories.
Prefer bugfix, SEO/content, infrastructure, docs, grant, and academic-outreach support work.
Do not treat GitHub Issues as primary progress tracking.
Require a morning next action for every story that reaches ready, blocked, or failed.
Prefer additive instrumentation and operator visibility before major workflow expansion.
Prefer disjoint file ownership per story so overnight lanes can run in parallel with minimal merge conflict.

stories.title: Define verified municipality demand signal
stories.description: Implement a first version of a verified resident demand signal that aggregates existing resident/formation events into a municipality-level metric the operator can inspect and reuse. This should live in the data/insights layer, not only in templates.
acceptanceCriteria: There is a clear function or query path that produces municipality-level resident demand output from existing system data.
The output distinguishes demand-oriented evidence from pure public-data heuristics.
The implementation is covered by tests for at least one positive and one empty/low-signal case.

stories.title: Expose municipality demand in /admin/strategy
stories.description: Update the operator/admin strategy surface so it shows verified demand context per municipality and makes it obvious which municipalities should be acted on next. Keep ownership primarily in the admin route/template path so this lane stays separate from outreach and ranking work.
acceptanceCriteria: An admin/operator surface renders municipality demand values or summaries from the new demand signal.
The surface highlights actionable municipalities instead of showing only generic pipeline numbers.
Tests cover the rendering path or response contract for the demand-aware operator view.

stories.title: Incorporate verified demand into municipality targeting
stories.description: Extend municipality prioritization so verified demand can influence outreach targeting alongside existing energy-transition and value-gap heuristics. Keep ownership primarily in targeting/scoring code so this lane can run independently of admin and copy changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Municipality targeting code can consume the verified demand signal.
Demand changes affect ranking or prioritization output in a documented way.
Tests cover at least one ranking scenario where higher demand changes municipality priority.

stories.title: Generate a municipality outreach pack from demand context
stories.description: Update outreach generation so municipality emails or operator-facing outreach artifacts include a concise demand-aware context block sourced from the municipality demand snapshot. Keep ownership primarily in `email_automation.py` and outreach templates so this lane can run in parallel with admin and ranking work.
acceptanceCriteria: Outreach generation has access to municipality demand context.
The rendered outreach content includes demand-aware justification or summary.
Tests cover rendering/generation with and without available demand context.

stories.title: Add operator-facing next-action summaries from demand context
stories.description: Add concise next-action summaries to operator-facing strategy output so the demand signal and targeting score resolve into a concrete recommendation per municipality. Keep this as a presentation/integration lane after admin demand rendering and targeting logic are both in place.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one operator-facing surface or response includes a concrete next-action summary derived from demand plus targeting context.
The new output emphasizes municipality action instead of generic analytics.
Tests still pass after the response/template change.

stories.title: Align public and operator messaging to the municipality-demand sequence
stories.description: Tighten high-leverage messaging surfaces so they reinforce the strategy sequence of public data -> resident demand capture -> municipality action, instead of diffusing attention across unrelated narratives. Keep this lane mostly in templates/copy so it can follow the functional changes.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one public or operator-facing template is updated to reflect the municipality-demand sequence.
The new copy avoids over-emphasizing speculative strategy themes for this workflow.
Tests still pass after the copy/template change.
